matches.x: 1-2 Moroni knew that Ammoron had a perfect knowledge of his fraud. Moroni became more incensed at Ammoron's perfidy when he received the King's latest epistle. He knew that no man, however depraved he was, or how diseased his heart, especially one who had been a Nephite, could resort to such accusations as did Ammoron without knowing them to be false. What seemed to rouse Moroni's wrath to the…

3-15 I know the place where the Lamanites guard my people whom they have taken prisoners. Release of the Nephite prisoners of war held by the Lamanites, and a safe return of them to their own people, was an objective of prime importance to Moroni. For him to make an exchange with the Lamanites of Lamanitish prisoners he had taken, for Nephite prisoners, man for man, was not a practical thing to…

Be Cautious That No Poison Is Administered among You Moroni refuses to exchange prisoners-The Lamanite guards are enticed to become drunk, and the Nephite prisoners are freed-The city of Gid is taken without bloodshed (Alma 55 headnote). After Moroni received Ammoron's reply and read his justification for attacking the Nephites, he was even angrier. He then decided to get the Nephite prisoners…

known unto them, according to their desires, and their faith, and prayers, of that which has been, and which is, and which is to come;

Nabal’s heart was merry within him, for he was very drunken; wherefore she told him nothing, less or more, until the morning light.

And there was also a cause of much sorrow among the Lamanites; for behold, they had many children
And there was also a cause of much sorrow among the Lamanites; for behold, they had many children

24-25 Moroni took possession of the City of Gid. Moroni's objective in taking the City of Gid was fulfilled, and his greater aim in doing so without bloodshed was tantamount to several victories in the field. In doing so he lost no men, but gained the release of the Nephite prisoners many of whom joined his forces, and, also, he took numerous Lamanite prisoners himself. The Lamanites whom Moroni…
